description: Thematic A1 lessons for LuxLingo — traditional situational content

thematic_lessons.situation: Meeting someone for the first time or seeing someone you know

vocabulary.notes: Most common Luxembourgish greeting, used any time of day
More formal than 'Moien'; 'Gudde' is accusative of 'gutt'
Very common informal farewell in Luxembourgish
Formal 'Dir' — used with adults you don't know well
'Merci' is universally used in Luxembourg for 'thank you'
Used with country names: Ech kommen aus Däitschland
Capital D; used with strangers and in formal contexts

sentences.text_lu: Moien!
sentences.text_en: Hello!

sentences.text_lu: Gudde Moien, Här Weiss!
sentences.text_en: Good morning, Mr. Weiss!

sentences.text_lu: Äddi, Marc!
sentences.text_en: Goodbye, Marc!

sentences.text_lu: Wéi heeschs du?
sentences.text_en: What is your name?

sentences.text_lu: Ech heeschen Anna.
sentences.text_en: My name is Anna.

sentences.text_lu: Wéi geet et?
sentences.text_en: How are you?

sentences.text_lu: Gutt, Merci!
sentences.text_en: Good, thank you!

sentences.text_lu: Ech kommen aus Belsch.
sentences.text_en: I come from Belgium.

sentences.text_lu: Wou kënns du hier?
sentences.text_en: Where are you from?

sentences.text_lu: Ech sinn eelef Joer al.
sentences.text_en: I am eleven years old.

sentences.text_lu: Frou ze treffen, Lena!
sentences.text_en: Nice to meet you, Lena!

sentences.text_lu: Servus, Paul!
sentences.text_en: Hi, Paul!

sentences.text_lu: Gudden Owend, wéi heescht Dir?
sentences.text_en: Good evening, what is your name?

sentences.text_lu: Ech heeschen Claire a ech kommen aus Lëtzebuerg.
sentences.text_en: My name is Claire and I come from Luxembourg.

sentences.text_lu: Wéi al bass du, Marc? Ech sinn aacht Joer al.
sentences.text_en: How old are you, Marc? I am eight years old.

sentences.text_lu: Dat ass meng Frëndin Natali. Si wunnt nieft eis.
sentences.text_en: This is my friend Natali. She lives next to us.

sentences.text_lu: Wéi geet et Iech, Här Weiss? Et geet mir gutt, Merci.
sentences.text_en: How are you, Mr. Weiss? I am well, thank you.

sentences.text_lu: Anna a Paul kommen aus Däitschland, awer si wunnen elo zu Lëtzebuerg.
sentences.text_en: Anna and Paul come from Germany, but they now live in Luxembourg.

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Moien! Ech heeschen Claire. Wéi heeschs du?
lines.en: Hello! My name is Claire. What is your name?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Moien! Ech heeschen Anna. Frou ze treffen!
lines.en: Hello! My name is Anna. Nice to meet you!

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Wou kënns du hier, Anna?
lines.en: Where are you from, Anna?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Ech kommen aus Däitschland. A du?
lines.en: I come from Germany. And you?

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Ech kommen aus Lëtzebuerg. Wéi al bass du?
lines.en: I come from Luxembourg. How old are you?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Ech sinn eelef Joer al. A du?
lines.en: I am eleven years old. And you?

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Ech sinn och eelef. Äddi, Anna!
lines.en: I am also eleven. Goodbye, Anna!

lines.speaker: Här Weiss
lines.lu: Gudde Moien! Wéi heescht Dir?
lines.en: Good morning! What is your name?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Gudde Moien, Här Weiss. Ech heeschen Paul.
lines.en: Good morning, Mr. Weiss. My name is Paul.

lines.speaker: Här Weiss
lines.lu: Wéi al sidd Dir, Paul?
lines.en: How old are you, Paul?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Ech sinn zwielef Joer al.
lines.en: I am twelve years old.

lines.speaker: Här Weiss
lines.lu: Frou ze treffen, Paul. Wou kommt Dir hier?
lines.en: Nice to meet you, Paul. Where do you come from?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Ech kommen aus Däitschland, Här Weiss.
lines.en: I come from Germany, Mr. Weiss.

vocabulary.notes: 'eent' standalone; 'een' before masculine/neuter nouns, 'eng' before feminine

sentences.text_lu: Eent, zwee, dräi!
sentences.text_en: One, two, three!

sentences.text_lu: Ech sinn aacht Joer al.
sentences.text_en: I am eight years old.

sentences.text_lu: Wéi vill Kanner sinn am Klass?
sentences.text_en: How many children are in the class?

sentences.text_lu: Et sinn zwanzeg Kanner.
sentences.text_en: There are twenty children.

sentences.text_lu: Dat kascht fënnef Euro.
sentences.text_en: That costs five euros.

sentences.text_lu: Meng Telefonsnummer ass null-zéng-zwanzeg-drësseg.
sentences.text_en: My phone number is 0-10-20-30.

sentences.text_lu: Et gi siwen Deeg an enger Woch.
sentences.text_en: There are seven days in a week.

sentences.text_lu: Ech hunn zwee Geschwëster.
sentences.text_en: I have two siblings.

sentences.text_lu: Dat kascht véierzeg Euro a fofzeg Cent.
sentences.text_en: That costs forty euros and fifty cents.

sentences.text_lu: Marc huet aacht Croissanten a se kaschteg dräi Euro.
sentences.text_en: Marc has eight croissants and they cost three euros.

sentences.text_lu: Wéi vill kascht dat? — Et kascht nonzeg-néng Euro.
sentences.text_en: How much does that cost? — It costs ninety-nine euros.

sentences.text_lu: An der Schoul sinn honnert Kanner.
sentences.text_en: There are one hundred children in the school.

sentences.text_lu: Natali ass fënnef Joer al an hir Schwëster ass siwwenzéng.
sentences.text_en: Natali is five years old and her sister is seventeen.

lines.speaker: Verkeefer
lines.lu: Moien! Wéi vill Äppel wëllt Dir?
lines.en: Good morning! How many apples would you like?

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.lu: Moien! Ech hätt gär sechs Äppel.
lines.en: Good morning! I would like six apples.

lines.speaker: Verkeefer
lines.lu: Sechs Äppel kaschtege véier Euro.
lines.en: Six apples cost four euros.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.lu: An wéi vill kascht eng Banann?
lines.en: And how much does a banana cost?

lines.speaker: Verkeefer
lines.lu: Eng Banann kascht drësseg Cent.
lines.en: A banana costs thirty cents.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.lu: Gutt, ech huelen dräi Banannen och. Dat sinn zesumme fënnef Euro zéng.
lines.en: Good, I'll take three bananas too. That's five euros ten altogether.

thematic_lessons.situation: Talking about dates, floors, rankings, order

sentences.text_lu: Haut ass den éischten Oktober.
sentences.text_en: Today is the first of October.

sentences.text_lu: Claire ass déi Éischt an der Klass.
sentences.text_en: Claire is first in the class.

sentences.text_lu: Ech wunnen am zweeten Etage.
sentences.text_en: I live on the second floor.

sentences.text_lu: Den éischten Dag vun der Schoul ass wichteg.
sentences.text_en: The first day of school is important.

sentences.text_lu: De wéivielten ass haut?
sentences.text_en: What is today's date?

sentences.text_lu: Haut ass den drëtten Mee.
sentences.text_en: Today is the third of May.

sentences.text_lu: Paul ass de Zweeten an der Rennen.
sentences.text_en: Paul is second in the race.

sentences.text_lu: Den Opzug fiert bis an den zéngten Etage.
sentences.text_en: The lift goes up to the tenth floor.

sentences.text_lu: D'Schoul fänkt den néngte September un.
sentences.text_en: School starts on the ninth of September.

sentences.text_lu: Den zwanzegste Mäerz ass den éischten Dag vum Fréijoer.
sentences.text_en: The twentieth of March is the first day of spring.

sentences.text_lu: Marc ass den Éischten am Maths, awer de Zweeten am Franséisch.
sentences.text_en: Marc is first in maths, but second in French.

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: De wéivielten ass haut, Claire?
lines.en: What is today's date, Claire?

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Haut ass den fofzéngten November.
lines.en: Today is the fifteenth of November.

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: An wéi vill Deeg ass Chrëschtdag?
lines.en: And in how many days is Christmas?

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Den Chrëschtdag ass den fënnefanzwanzegsten Dezember. Dat sinn nach zéng Wochen!
lines.en: Christmas is the twenty-fifth of December. That's still ten weeks!

vocabulary.notes: Attributive: blo stays blo in most contexts; blouën before noun

sentences.text_lu: D'Himmel ass blo.
sentences.text_en: The sky is blue.

sentences.text_lu: D'Gras ass gréng.
sentences.text_en: The grass is green.

sentences.text_lu: D'Sonn ass giel.
sentences.text_en: The sun is yellow.

sentences.text_lu: Bello ass e brong an e wäiss Hond.
sentences.text_en: Bello is a brown and white dog.

sentences.text_lu: Meng Léiflingsfarrf ass blo.
sentences.text_en: My favourite colour is blue.

sentences.text_lu: Wéi eng Faarf ass dat?
sentences.text_en: What colour is that?

sentences.text_lu: Den Apel ass rout.
sentences.text_en: The apple is red.

sentences.text_lu: D'Kaart ass giel.
sentences.text_en: The card is yellow.

sentences.text_lu: Anna dreet e rouden Pullover an eng blo Jeans.
sentences.text_en: Anna is wearing a red pullover and blue jeans.

sentences.text_lu: Natali huet eng rosa Jackett a schwaarz Schong.
sentences.text_en: Natali has a pink jacket and black shoes.

sentences.text_lu: De Marc huet e bléiwen Auto — awer ech léiwer orange!
sentences.text_en: Marc has a blue car — but I prefer orange!

sentences.text_lu: D'Faarwen vun der Lëtzebuerger Fändel sinn rout, wäiss a hellblo.
sentences.text_en: The colours of the Luxembourg flag are red, white and light blue.

sentences.text_lu: Här Weiss dréit ëmmer e groen Kostüm an eng wäiss Krauatt.
sentences.text_en: Mr. Weiss always wears a grey suit and a white tie.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Lena, wéi eng Faarf huet deng Schoultas?
lines.en: Lena, what colour is your school bag?

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: Meng Schoultas ass lila. A deng, Paul?
lines.en: My school bag is purple. And yours, Paul?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Meng ass schwaarzmit engem blouën Stär.
lines.en: Mine is black with a blue star.

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: Cool! Meng Léiflingsfarrf ass lila. A deng?
lines.en: Cool! My favourite colour is purple. And yours?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Ech hunn gär gréng. Et ass d'Faarf vum Gazon!
lines.en: I like green. It's the colour of the grass!

thematic_lessons.situation: Talking about schedules, plans, and routines

sentences.text_lu: Haut ass Méindeg.
sentences.text_en: Today is Monday.

sentences.text_lu: Muer ass Dënschdeg.
sentences.text_en: Tomorrow is Tuesday.

sentences.text_lu: Gëschter war Sonndeg.
sentences.text_en: Yesterday was Sunday.

sentences.text_lu: Wéi en Dag ass haut?
sentences.text_en: What day is today?

sentences.text_lu: D'Schoul ass zou de Samschdeg an de Sonndeg.
sentences.text_en: School is closed on Saturday and Sunday.

sentences.text_lu: Ech hunn all Méindeg Schwammstonn.
sentences.text_en: I have swimming class every Monday.

sentences.text_lu: De Freideg ass mäin Léiflingsdag!
sentences.text_en: Friday is my favourite day!

sentences.text_lu: Am Wochend schlofen ech laang.
sentences.text_en: At the weekend I sleep in.

sentences.text_lu: De Mëttwoch ginn ech mat Marc Fussball spillen.
sentences.text_en: On Wednesday I go to play football with Marc.

sentences.text_lu: Här Weiss gëtt iwwermuer kee Cours, well en e Rendez-vous huet.
sentences.text_en: Mr. Weiss has no class the day after tomorrow because he has an appointment.

sentences.text_lu: Lena kënnt e Samschdeg bei eis spillen — mir sinn och doheem.
sentences.text_en: Lena is coming to play at our place on Saturday — we are at home too.

sentences.text_lu: D'Anna huet donneschdeg Fussball, freides Danzstonnden a samschdes Gitarstonnden.
sentences.text_en: Anna has football on Thursday, dance lessons on Friday and guitar on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Wéi en Dag ass haut, Paul?
lines.en: What day is today, Paul?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Haut ass Dënschdeg. Muer ass Mëttwoch.
lines.en: Today is Tuesday. Tomorrow is Wednesday.

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Super! Wëlls du e Mëttwoch mat mir Fussball spillen?
lines.en: Great! Do you want to play football with me on Wednesday?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Jo, gär! A e Samschdeg? Wat méchs du da?
lines.en: Yes, gladly! And on Saturday? What are you doing then?

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: E Samschdeg ginn ech mam Papp an de Supermarché. Awer e Sonndeg si mir fräi.
lines.en: On Saturday I go to the supermarket with my dad. But on Sunday we are free.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Perfekt, mir kënnen e Sonndeg och spillen!
lines.en: Perfect, we can also play on Sunday!

thematic_lessons.situation: Stating and asking about dates, birthdays, appointments

vocabulary.notes: Masc: den Abrëll (n-rule: starts with vowel)
Masc: den August (n-rule: starts with vowel)

sentences.text_lu: De Mount Januar ass kal.
sentences.text_en: The month of January is cold.

sentences.text_lu: Meng Gebuertsdag ass de véierten Juli.
sentences.text_en: My birthday is the fourth of July.

sentences.text_lu: Wéini ass deng Gebuertsdag?
sentences.text_en: When is your birthday?

sentences.text_lu: D'Schoul fänkt am September un.
sentences.text_en: School starts in September.

sentences.text_lu: De wéivielten ass haut?
sentences.text_en: What is today's date?

sentences.text_lu: Haut ass den zwanzegste Mee.
sentences.text_en: Today is the twentieth of May.

sentences.text_lu: Am Dezember ass et Chrëschtdag.
sentences.text_en: In December it is Christmas.

sentences.text_lu: Et gi zwielef Méint am Joer.
sentences.text_en: There are twelve months in a year.

sentences.text_lu: Marc ass den aachten August gebuer — dat ass am Summer.
sentences.text_en: Marc was born on the eighth of August — that is in summer.

sentences.text_lu: Lena huet hiren Anniversaire den éischten Abrëll — all Joer maache mir eng Party!
sentences.text_en: Lena has her birthday on the first of April — every year we have a party!

sentences.text_lu: D'Vakanze fänken de fofzéngten Juli un a ginn bis den drëssegsten August.
sentences.text_en: The holidays start on the fifteenth of July and go until the thirtieth of August.

sentences.text_lu: Nächst Joer am Februar goe mir Ski fueren an den Alpen.
sentences.text_en: Next year in February we are going skiing in the Alps.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Wéini ass deng Gebuertsdag, Natali?
lines.en: When is your birthday, Natali?

lines.speaker: Natali
lines.lu: Meng Gebuertsdag ass den zweete Mäerz. A wéini ass déngen, Anna?
lines.en: My birthday is the second of March. And when is yours, Anna?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Meng Gebuertsdag ass den fofzéngten Oktober. Ech ginn dëst Joer zwielef Joer.
lines.en: My birthday is the fifteenth of October. I will be twelve years old this year.

lines.speaker: Natali
lines.lu: Mir feieren zesummen! Ech ginn de Mäerz sechs Joer.
lines.en: We will celebrate together! I will be six years old in March.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Super! Ech froen d'Mamm ob mir eng Gebuertsdeesparty maache kënnen.
lines.en: Great! I will ask mum whether we can have a birthday party.

thematic_lessons.situation: Talking about seasons, the weather forecast, and outdoor plans

vocabulary.notes: Masc: den Hierscht (n-rule: starts with H)
Es sinn zwanzeg Grad = it is twenty degrees

vocabulary.meaning_en: What is the weather like today?

sentences.text_lu: Et reent haut.
sentences.text_en: It is raining today.

sentences.text_lu: D'Sonn schéngt am Summer.
sentences.text_en: The sun shines in summer.

sentences.text_lu: Et schneit am Wanter.
sentences.text_en: It snows in winter.

sentences.text_lu: Wéi ass d'Wieder haut?
sentences.text_en: What is the weather like today?

sentences.text_lu: Et ass zéng Grad kal.
sentences.text_en: It is ten degrees cold.

sentences.text_lu: Am Hierscht falen d'Blieder vun de Beem.
sentences.text_en: In autumn the leaves fall from the trees.

sentences.text_lu: De Wand bléist staark haut.
sentences.text_en: The wind is blowing strongly today.

sentences.text_lu: Et ass schéi Wieder am Fréijoer.
sentences.text_en: The weather is nice in spring.

sentences.text_lu: Haut ass et bewölkt a vläicht reent et nomëtteg.
sentences.text_en: Today it is cloudy and perhaps it will rain in the afternoon.

sentences.text_lu: Am Summer gëtt et bis drësseg Grad waarm zu Lëtzebuerg.
sentences.text_en: In summer it gets up to thirty degrees warm in Luxembourg.

sentences.text_lu: Marc léiwer de Summer, well hie gär schwamme geet, awer Natali léiwer de Wanter wéinst dem Schnéi.
sentences.text_en: Marc prefers summer because he likes going swimming, but Natali prefers winter because of the snow.

sentences.text_lu: Wann d'Sonn schéngt, goe mir an de Park; wann et reent, bleiwe mir doheem.
sentences.text_en: When the sun shines, we go to the park; when it rains, we stay at home.

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: Moien, Anna! Wéi ass d'Wieder haut?
lines.en: Good morning, Anna! What is the weather like today?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Moien, Lena! Et ass kal a bewölkt. Et kéint souguer schneien!
lines.en: Good morning, Lena! It is cold and cloudy. It could even snow!

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: Oh nee! Ech hunn keng Handsche matgeholl.
lines.en: Oh no! I didn't bring any gloves.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Ech hunn eng Extrapaor. Hei, huelt déi!
lines.en: I have a spare pair. Here, take these!

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: Merci vill Mol! Wéi eng Temperatur ass et?
lines.en: Thank you very much! What is the temperature?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Et sinn ongeféier dräi Grad. Am Summer ass et vill besser — drësseg Grad!
lines.en: It is approximately three degrees. In summer it is much better — thirty degrees!

thematic_lessons.situation: Asking and telling the time, talking about schedules

vocabulary.notes: The standard way to ask the time
hallwer dräi = half past two (literally 'half three')
Véiertel op dräi = quarter past three
Véiertel vir dräi = quarter to three
Zéng Minutten op véier = ten past four
Fënnef Minutten vir sechs = five to six
Ëm néng Auer = at nine o'clock

sentences.text_lu: Wéi vill Auer ass et?
sentences.text_en: What time is it?

sentences.text_lu: Et ass zéng Auer.
sentences.text_en: It is ten o'clock.

sentences.text_lu: Et ass hallwer aacht.
sentences.text_en: It is half past seven.

sentences.text_lu: Et ass Véiertel op néng.
sentences.text_en: It is quarter past eight.

sentences.text_lu: Et ass Véiertel vir zwielef.
sentences.text_en: It is quarter to twelve.

sentences.text_lu: D'Schoul fänkt ëm aacht Auer un.
sentences.text_en: School starts at eight o'clock.

sentences.text_lu: Ech iessen mëtteg ëm eelef Auer.
sentences.text_en: I eat lunch at eleven o'clock.

sentences.text_lu: Den Zuch fiert ëm dräi Auer of.
sentences.text_en: The train departs at three o'clock.

sentences.text_lu: Et ass zéng Minutten op siwen moies — ech muss séier goen!
sentences.text_en: It is ten past seven in the morning — I have to go quickly!

sentences.text_lu: De Film fänkt ëm hallwer néng owends un a geet bis zwielef Auer nuets.
sentences.text_en: The film starts at half past eight in the evening and goes until twelve o'clock at night.

sentences.text_lu: Här Weiss kommt ëmmer pünktlech ëm aacht Auer an d'Klass.
sentences.text_en: Mr. Weiss always arrives punctually at eight o'clock to the classroom.

sentences.text_lu: Marc ass ze spéit komm — et war scho Véiertel op néng!
sentences.text_en: Marc arrived too late — it was already quarter past eight!

dialogues.title: What time does the bus leave?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Entschëllegt, wéi vill Auer ass et?
lines.en: Excuse me, what time is it?

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.lu: Et ass Véiertel vir siwen.
lines.en: It is quarter to seven.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Oh! Wéini fiert den nächste Bus of?
lines.en: Oh! When does the next bus leave?

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.lu: De Bus fiert ëm siwen Auer of — an zwielef Minutten.
lines.en: The bus leaves at seven o'clock — in twelve minutes.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Super, Merci! Ech sinn nach pünktlech!
lines.en: Great, thank you! I am still on time!

thematic_lessons.situation: Describing your family members and relationships

sentences.text_lu: Ech hunn eng Schwëster an e Brudder.
sentences.text_en: I have a sister and a brother.

sentences.text_lu: Meng Mamm heescht Paula.
sentences.text_en: My mother's name is Paula.

sentences.text_lu: De Papp schafft zu Lëtzebuerg.
sentences.text_en: Dad works in Luxembourg.

sentences.text_lu: Hues du Geschwëster?
sentences.text_en: Do you have siblings?

sentences.text_lu: Ech sinn Eenzelkand.
sentences.text_en: I am an only child.

sentences.text_lu: D'Groussmamm wunnt bei eis.
sentences.text_en: Grandma lives with us.

sentences.text_lu: Meng Tatta huet zwee Kanner.
sentences.text_en: My aunt has two children.

sentences.text_lu: De Grousspapp spillt gär Schach.
sentences.text_en: Grandpa likes playing chess.

sentences.text_lu: Anna a Paul sinn Geschwëster — si kommen aus Däitschland.
sentences.text_en: Anna and Paul are siblings — they come from Germany.

sentences.text_lu: Meng Famill ass net grouss: meng Mamm, meng Papp, meng Schwëster a mech.
sentences.text_en: My family is not big: my mum, my dad, my sister and me.

sentences.text_lu: De Marc säin Monni brécht ëmmer eng Kéis mat wann hien ze Besuch kënnt.
sentences.text_en: Marc's uncle always brings a cheese when he comes to visit.

sentences.text_lu: D'Groussmamm vum Marc geet all Sonndeg mat him an d'Kiirch.
sentences.text_en: Marc's grandmother goes to church with him every Sunday.

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Anna, hues du Geschwëster?
lines.en: Anna, do you have siblings?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Jo, ech hunn ee Brudder. Hie heescht Paul a sinn zwielef Joer.
lines.en: Yes, I have one brother. His name is Paul and he is twelve years old.

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Ech och! Meng Schwëster heescht Sophie. A wou wunnen Är Grousselleren?
lines.en: Me too! My sister's name is Sophie. And where do your grandparents live?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: D'Groussellere vum Papp wunnen nach zu Köln. D'Groussellere vun der Mamm wunnen hei zu Lëtzebuerg.
lines.en: Dad's parents still live in Cologne. Mum's parents live here in Luxembourg.

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Kënns du deng Groussellere oft gesinn?
lines.en: Do you get to see your grandparents often?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: D'Bomi a de Bopi hei gesinn ech all Weekend. D'Groussellere zu Köln nëmmen an de Vakanze.
lines.en: I see grandma and grandpa here every weekend. The grandparents in Cologne only during holidays.

thematic_lessons.situation: Describing where you live — city, type of home, neighbourhood

vocabulary.notes: Ech wunnen, du wunns, hien/si wunnt
'zu' is used before city names in Luxembourgish

sentences.text_lu: Ech wunnen zu Lëtzebuerg.
sentences.text_en: I live in Luxembourg.

sentences.text_lu: Wou wunns du?
sentences.text_en: Where do you live?

sentences.text_lu: Ech wunnen an enger Wunneng.
sentences.text_en: I live in a flat.

sentences.text_lu: Mir wunnen an engem grousse Haus um Land.
sentences.text_en: We live in a big house in the countryside.

sentences.text_lu: D'Schoul ass ganz no bei eisem Haus.
sentences.text_en: The school is very close to our house.

sentences.text_lu: Natali wunnt nieft Anna.
sentences.text_en: Natali lives next to Anna.

sentences.text_lu: A wéi engem Quartier wunns du?
sentences.text_en: In which neighbourhood do you live?

sentences.text_lu: Ech wunnen an der Stad, no beim Bahnhof.
sentences.text_en: I live in the city centre, near the train station.

sentences.text_lu: Paul a seng Famill wunnen zu Esch-Uelzecht an enger grousser Wunneng am drëtten Etage.
sentences.text_en: Paul and his family live in Esch-sur-Alzette in a large flat on the third floor.

sentences.text_lu: Meng Adress ass: Buergertunnel 14, L-1247 Lëtzebuerg.
sentences.text_en: My address is: Buergertunnel 14, L-1247 Luxembourg.

sentences.text_lu: D'Lena wunnt zu Déifferdeng, dat ass ongeféier zwanzeg Kilometer vun Lëtzebuerg Stad ewech.
sentences.text_en: Lena lives in Differdange, which is approximately twenty kilometres from Luxembourg City.

sentences.text_lu: Am Quartier wou ech wunnen, gëtt et en Epicerie, eng Bäckerei an e Park — alles ganz no!
sentences.text_en: In the neighbourhood where I live, there is a grocery shop, a bakery and a park — all very nearby!

lines.speaker: Här Weiss
lines.lu: Paul, wou wunnt Dir zu Lëtzebuerg?
lines.en: Paul, where do you live in Luxembourg?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Ech wunnen zu Esch-Uelzecht, Här Weiss.
lines.en: I live in Esch-sur-Alzette, Mr. Weiss.

lines.speaker: Här Weiss
lines.lu: An wunnt Dir an engem Haus oder enger Wunneng?
lines.en: And do you live in a house or a flat?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Mir wunnen an enger Wunneng am zweeten Etage. Mir hunn ee schéine Balkon.
lines.en: We live in a flat on the second floor. We have a lovely balcony.

lines.speaker: Här Weiss
lines.lu: Ass d'Schoul wäit vun Ärem Haus?
lines.en: Is the school far from your home?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Nee, si ass ganz no. Ech ginn all Dag ze Fouss.
lines.en: No, it is very close. I walk every day.

vocabulary.notes: Ech schaffen, du schaffs, hien/si schafft
In Luxembourgish no article needed: Ech sinn Dokter

sentences.text_lu: Ech sinn Dokter.
sentences.text_en: I am a doctor.

sentences.text_lu: Meng Mamm ass Léiererin.
sentences.text_en: My mother is a teacher.

sentences.text_lu: Wou schafft däi Papp?
sentences.text_en: Where does your father work?

sentences.text_lu: Hie schafft an engem Büro zu Lëtzebuerg.
sentences.text_en: He works in an office in Luxembourg.

sentences.text_lu: Wat bass du vu Beruff?
sentences.text_en: What is your profession?

sentences.text_lu: Här Weiss ass Léierer an enger Schoul.
sentences.text_en: Mr. Weiss is a teacher at a school.

sentences.text_lu: De Pompjee schafft de Nuecht.
sentences.text_en: The firefighter works at night.

sentences.text_lu: D'Infirmière schafft am Spidol.
sentences.text_en: The nurse works in the hospital.

sentences.text_lu: Meng Mamm schafft Deelzäit als Bäckerin — si fänkt all Dag ëm véier Auer moies un.
sentences.text_en: My mother works part-time as a baker — she starts every day at four in the morning.

sentences.text_lu: De Papp vum Marc ass Ingenieur a schafft bei enger grousser Firma zu Lëtzebuerg.
sentences.text_en: Marc's father is an engineer and works at a large company in Luxembourg.

sentences.text_lu: Wann ech grouss sinn, wëll ech Dokter ginn — ech hëllefen gär de Leit.
sentences.text_en: When I grow up, I want to become a doctor — I like helping people.

sentences.text_lu: Zu Lëtzebuerg schaffe vill Leit an der Banquen- oder Finanzbranche.
sentences.text_en: In Luxembourg many people work in the banking or finance sector.

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: Claire, wat mécht deng Mamm vu Beruff?
lines.en: Claire, what does your mother do for a living?

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Meng Mamm ass Doktesch. Si schafft am Spidol. A deng?
lines.en: My mother is a doctor. She works in the hospital. And yours?

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: Meng Mamm ass Coiffeuse. Si huet hire Salon zu Déifferdeng.
lines.en: My mother is a hairdresser. She has her salon in Differdange.

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Schéin! A wat mécht däi Papp?
lines.en: Nice! And what does your father do?

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: De Papp ass Pompjee. Hie schafft och de Nuecht, dat ass spannend!
lines.en: Dad is a firefighter. He also works at night — that is exciting!

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Jo, wéi cool! Wann ech grouss sinn, wëll ech och Doktesch ginn.
lines.en: Yes, how cool! When I grow up, I also want to become a doctor.

thematic_lessons.situation: Asking for and giving directions in the city

vocabulary.notes: No d' here — lénks is invariable
Used to get attention before asking

vocabulary.meaning_en: The first street on the left.

sentences.text_lu: Wou ass d'Busstation?
sentences.text_en: Where is the bus stop?

sentences.text_lu: Gitt riets.
sentences.text_en: Go right.

sentences.text_lu: D'Kierch ass lénks.
sentences.text_en: The church is on the left.

sentences.text_lu: De Supermarché ass géintiwwer dem Bahnhof.
sentences.text_en: The supermarket is opposite the train station.

sentences.text_lu: Entschëllegt, wou ass d'Apothéik?
sentences.text_en: Excuse me, where is the pharmacy?

sentences.text_lu: Gitt gerausaus bis zur Ampel.
sentences.text_en: Go straight ahead as far as the traffic lights.

sentences.text_lu: De Park ass hannert der Schoul.
sentences.text_en: The park is behind the school.

sentences.text_lu: D'éischt Stroos lénks, dann ëm d'Eck.
sentences.text_en: The first street on the left, then around the corner.

sentences.text_lu: Entschëllegt! Wou ass de Bahnhof? — Gitt gerausaus, dann d'zweet Stroos riets, ganz no bei.
sentences.text_en: Excuse me! Where is the train station? — Go straight ahead, then the second street on the right, very nearby.

sentences.text_lu: D'Bäckerei ass tëscht dem Supermarché an der Apothéik.
sentences.text_en: The bakery is between the supermarket and the pharmacy.

sentences.text_lu: Marc weist Anna de Wee zur Schoul, well si déi nei Schülerin ass.
sentences.text_en: Marc shows Anna the way to school because she is the new pupil.

sentences.text_lu: Wann Dir aus dem Bahnhof erauskënnt, gitt lénks, dann direkt riets — d'Post ass ëm d'Eck.
sentences.text_en: When you come out of the train station, go left, then immediately right — the post office is around the corner.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Entschëllegt, wou ass d'Busstation, w.e.g.?
lines.en: Excuse me, where is the bus stop, please?

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.lu: Kee Problem! Gitt gerausaus bis zur Ampel.
lines.en: No problem! Go straight ahead as far as the traffic lights.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: D'éischt Stroos lénks oder riets?
lines.en: The first street on the left or on the right?

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.lu: Riets. Da siitt Dir d'Busstation direkt virun Iech.
lines.en: On the right. Then you will see the bus stop directly in front of you.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Ass et wäit ewech?
lines.en: Is it far away?

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.lu: Nee, ganz no — ongeféier fënnef Minutten ze Fouss.
lines.en: No, very close — approximately five minutes on foot.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Super, Merci vill Mol!
lines.en: Great, thank you very much!

thematic_lessons.situation: Getting around Luxembourg — public transport, tickets, asking about routes

vocabulary.notes: Masc; Luxembourg City has a modern tram network

vocabulary.meaning_en: How do you get to work?

vocabulary.notes: 'mat' + dative: mam Bus, mam Zuch, mam Auto
Masc; free public transport in Luxembourg since 2020!
Public transport in Luxembourg is free!
Separable: Den Zuch geet ëm néng Auer of.
Separable: Ech kommen ëm zéng Auer un.
Separable: Ech steige zu Miersch ëm.

vocabulary.word_lu: Wéini fiert den nächste Bus of?
vocabulary.meaning_en: When does the next bus leave?

sentences.text_lu: Ech fueren mam Bus op d'Aarbecht.
sentences.text_en: I travel to work by bus.

sentences.text_lu: De Tram fiert duerch d'Stad.
sentences.text_en: The tram goes through the city.

sentences.text_lu: Ech ginn ze Fouss op d'Schoul.
sentences.text_en: I walk to school.

sentences.text_lu: Wéini fiert den nächste Zuch of?
sentences.text_en: When does the next train leave?

sentences.text_lu: De Bus fiert ëm hallwer néng of.
sentences.text_en: The bus leaves at half past eight.

sentences.text_lu: Zu Lëtzebuerg ass de public Transport gratis!
sentences.text_en: In Luxembourg public transport is free!

sentences.text_lu: Wéi kënns du op d'Aarbecht, mam Auto oder mam Zuch?
sentences.text_en: How do you get to work, by car or by train?

sentences.text_lu: D'Haltestell ass just ëm d'Eck.
sentences.text_en: The stop is just around the corner.

sentences.text_lu: Anna a Paul fueren all Moien mam Schoulbus — hie kënnt ëm aacht Auer.
sentences.text_en: Anna and Paul take the school bus every morning — it arrives at eight o'clock.

sentences.text_lu: Ech steige zu Miersch aus dem Zuch ëm an den Bus Richtung Diekirch.
sentences.text_en: I change at Mersch from the train to the bus towards Diekirch.

sentences.text_lu: Wéinst dem Stau dauert d'Fuert mam Auto vun Esch bis an d'Stad eng Stonn.
sentences.text_en: Because of the traffic jam, the drive by car from Esch to the city takes one hour.

sentences.text_lu: Mir kucken de Fahrplo: den nächste Zuch no Lëtzebuerg Gare fiert ëm Véiertel vir néng of.
sentences.text_en: We look at the timetable: the next train to Luxembourg station leaves at quarter to nine.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Paul, wéi gees du all Dag op d'Schoul?
lines.en: Paul, how do you get to school every day?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Ech fueren mam Bus. Hie fiert ëm Véiertel op aacht of. A du?
lines.en: I go by bus. It leaves at quarter past seven. And you?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Ech ginn ze Fouss — eis Schoul ass just fënnef Minutten ewech.
lines.en: I walk — our school is just five minutes away.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Schéin! Mäin Bus brauch fofzéng Minutten. Muer fuere mir zesummen?
lines.en: Nice! My bus takes fifteen minutes. Shall we go together tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.lu: Jo, gär! Ech kommen bei d'Haltestell ëm aacht Auer.
lines.en: Yes, gladly! I will come to the stop at eight o'clock.

thematic_lessons.situation: Ordering food and drinks at a café or restaurant

vocabulary.notes: Fem; eng Taass Kaffi = a cup of coffee
Polite way to order; 'hätt' is conditional of 'hunn'

vocabulary.meaning_en: What can I get you? / What would you like?

vocabulary.notes: Universal in Luxembourg — used in all contexts

sentences.text_lu: Ech hätt gär eng Taass Kaffi.
sentences.text_en: I would like a cup of coffee.

sentences.text_lu: Wat däerf et sinn?
sentences.text_en: What can I get you?

sentences.text_lu: Ech hätt gär e Croissant, w.e.g.
sentences.text_en: I would like a croissant, please.

sentences.text_lu: D'Rechnung, w.e.g.!
sentences.text_en: The bill, please!

sentences.text_lu: Wéi vill kascht eng Taass Téi?
sentences.text_en: How much does a cup of tea cost?

sentences.text_lu: Ech wëll de Jus d'orange ouni Äis.
sentences.text_en: I want the orange juice without ice.

sentences.text_lu: Huet de Kaffi Mëllech?
sentences.text_en: Does the coffee have milk?

sentences.text_lu: Mir hätte gär zwee Waasser a Croissanten, w.e.g.
sentences.text_en: We would like two waters and croissants, please.

sentences.text_lu: Marc an Anna sëtze am Café a bestellen e Jus a Kuchen.
sentences.text_en: Marc and Anna are sitting in the café and ordering juice and cake.

sentences.text_lu: D'Kellnerin erkläert datt de Kuch vum Dag Äppelkuch ass — Marc bestellt een.
sentences.text_en: The waitress explains that today's cake is apple cake — Marc orders one.

sentences.text_lu: Ech hätt gär eng Taass Kaffi mat Mëllech awer ouni Zocker, w.e.g.
sentences.text_en: I would like a cup of coffee with milk but without sugar, please.

sentences.text_lu: D'Rechnung, w.e.g. — kascht dat fënnef oder sechs Euro?
sentences.text_en: The bill, please — does that cost five or six euros?

lines.speaker: Kellnerin
lines.lu: Gudde Mëtteg! Wat däerf et sinn?
lines.en: Good afternoon! What can I get you?

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Ech hätt gär eng Taass Téi mat Mëllech, w.e.g.
lines.en: I would like a cup of tea with milk, please.

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: A fir mech e Jus d'orange an e Croissant, Merci.
lines.en: And for me an orange juice and a croissant, thank you.

lines.speaker: Kellnerin
lines.lu: De Croissant ass mat oder ouni Botter?
lines.en: Is the croissant with or without butter?

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.lu: Mat Botter, w.e.g.!
lines.en: With butter, please!

lines.speaker: Kellnerin
lines.lu: Gutt, ech bréngen alles direkt.
lines.en: Good, I will bring everything right away.

lines.speaker: Claire
lines.lu: Merci vill Mol!
lines.en: Thank you very much!

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.lu: Entschëllegt, d'Rechnung, w.e.g.!
lines.en: Excuse me, the bill, please!

lines.speaker: Kellner
lines.lu: Jo, natierlech. Dat sinn aacht Euro fofzeg.
lines.en: Yes, of course. That is eight euros fifty.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.lu: Kann ech mat Kaart bezuelen?
lines.en: Can I pay by card?

lines.speaker: Kellner
lines.lu: Jo, kee Problem. Hei ass den Terminal.
lines.en: Yes, no problem. Here is the terminal.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.lu: Merci! Den Kaffi war exzellent!
lines.en: Thank you! The coffee was excellent!

thematic_lessons.situation: Shopping in a store, asking prices, paying

vocabulary.notes: Fem: eng Dosen Eeër = a dozen eggs

sentences.text_lu: Wéi vill kascht dat?
sentences.text_en: How much does that cost?

sentences.text_lu: Dat kascht dräi Euro fofzeg.
sentences.text_en: That costs three euros fifty.

sentences.text_lu: Dat ass ze deier!
sentences.text_en: That is too expensive!

sentences.text_lu: Kann ech mat Kaart bezuelen?
sentences.text_en: Can I pay by card?

sentences.text_lu: Ech sichen en T-Shirt a grénger Faarf.
sentences.text_en: I am looking for a T-shirt in green.

sentences.text_lu: D'Äppel si bëlleg haut — zwee Kilo fir zwee Euro.
sentences.text_en: The apples are cheap today — two kilos for two euros.

sentences.text_lu: Hutt Dir dës Schong an enger grousse Pointure?
sentences.text_en: Do you have these shoes in a larger size?

sentences.text_lu: D'Akafslëscht vum Marc ass laang: Brout, Mëllech, Eeër a Käse.
sentences.text_en: Marc's shopping list is long: bread, milk, eggs and cheese.

sentences.text_lu: Am Soldes kascht dee Mantel nëmmen dräizeg Euro — fir gewinnen honnert Euro!
sentences.text_en: In the sales that coat costs only thirty euros — normally one hundred euros!

sentences.text_lu: Natali hëlleeft hirer Mamm am Supermarché — si kucken op der Akafslëscht wat nach feelt.
sentences.text_en: Natali helps her mother in the supermarket — they check the shopping list for what is still missing.

sentences.text_lu: Kann ech mat Kaart bezuelen oder brauch ech Baargeld?
sentences.text_en: Can I pay by card or do I need cash?

sentences.text_lu: Den Präis vum Brout ass gestigen — d'lescht Joer kascht et nëmmen ee Euro, elo kascht et ee fofzeg.
sentences.text_en: The price of bread has risen — last year it cost only one euro, now it costs one fifty.

lines.speaker: Verkeefer
lines.lu: Gudde Moien! Wat däerf et sinn?
lines.en: Good morning! What can I get you?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Gudde Moien! Ech hätt gär eng Déck Botter a sechs Croissanten.
lines.en: Good morning! I would like a large loaf and six croissants.

lines.speaker: Verkeefer
lines.lu: Natierlech. Wëllt Dir e Brout mam Kären oder ouni?
lines.en: Of course. Do you want bread with seeds or without?

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Mat Kären, w.e.g. Wéi vill kascht dat alles?
lines.en: With seeds, please. How much does all that cost?

lines.speaker: Verkeefer
lines.lu: Dat sinn zesumme fënnef Euro zwanzeg.
lines.en: That is five euros twenty altogether.

lines.speaker: Paul
lines.lu: Hei sinn sechs Euro. Behalt de Rescht!
lines.en: Here is six euros. Keep the change!

lines.speaker: Verkeefer
lines.lu: Merci vill Mol! Schéine Dag nach!
lines.en: Thank you very much! Have a lovely day!

thematic_lessons.situation: Discussing the weather in detail, forecasts, and weather-related plans

vocabulary.notes: Et sinn dräi Grad ënner null.

vocabulary.meaning_en: to be safe / just in case

sentences.text_lu: Et reent haut, huel den Regenschirm mat!
sentences.text_en: It is raining today, take the umbrella!

sentences.text_lu: D'Sonn schéngt a muer gëtt et schéi.
sentences.text_en: The sun is shining and tomorrow it will be nice.

sentences.text_lu: Et donnert a blëtzt — dat ass e Stuerm!
sentences.text_en: It is thundering and lightning — that is a storm!

sentences.text_lu: Et sinn fënnef Grad ënner null — et ass Frascht!
sentences.text_en: It is five degrees below zero — there is frost!

sentences.text_lu: Wéi ass d'Wieder muer laut Wieder-Bericht?
sentences.text_en: What is the weather like tomorrow according to the forecast?

sentences.text_lu: D'Wolleken zéien op — vläicht reent et!
sentences.text_en: The clouds are gathering — maybe it will rain!

sentences.text_lu: Am Summer gëtt et zu Lëtzebuerg dacks feuchte Wieder.
sentences.text_en: In summer Luxembourg often has humid weather.

sentences.text_lu: Den Hierscht zu Lëtzebuerg ass dacks grau a rénegend.
sentences.text_en: Autumn in Luxembourg is often grey and rainy.

sentences.text_lu: Natali schwätzt mat hirem Papp iwwer d'Wieder: et gëtt nächst Woch Schnéi!
sentences.text_en: Natali talks with her dad about the weather: next week there will be snow!

sentences.text_lu: De Wieder-Bericht seet, et kënnt e Stuerm muer — d'Schoul kann zou sinn.
sentences.text_en: The weather forecast says there is a storm coming tomorrow — school might be closed.

sentences.text_lu: Am Wanter zu Lëtzebuerg geet d'Temperatur selten ënner minus zéng Grad, mee et kann däischter an naass ginn.
sentences.text_en: In winter in Luxembourg the temperature rarely goes below minus ten degrees, but it can get dark and wet.

sentences.text_lu: Bello läit doheem well et ze kal fir spillen dobaussen ass — et reent a blëtzt!
sentences.text_en: Bello is staying indoors because it is too cold to play outside — it is raining and lightning!

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.lu: Moien, Natali! Bass du sécher, datt d'Wieder haut schéi gëtt?
lines.en: Morning, Natali! Are you sure the weather will be nice today?

lines.speaker: Natali
lines.lu: Moien! Laut Wieder-Bericht schéngt d'Sonn nomëtteg. Moies ass et awer bewölkt.
lines.en: Morning! According to the forecast the sun will shine in the afternoon. In the morning it is cloudy though.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.lu: Wéi vill Grad sinn et?
lines.en: How many degrees is it?

lines.speaker: Natali
lines.lu: Just dräizéng Grad moies, awer nomëtteg gëtt et zwanzeg Grad.
lines.en: Only thirteen degrees in the morning, but in the afternoon it will reach twenty degrees.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.lu: Super! Da kënne mir nomëtteg am Park spillen!
lines.en: Great! Then we can play in the park in the afternoon!

lines.speaker: Natali
lines.lu: Jo! Huel awer sëcherheetshalwer eng Jackett mat — et ka séier kal ginn!
lines.en: Yes! But take a jacket just in case — it can get cold quickly!
